text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, tech-chased, everything else bled quietly. XLK ripped +2.48% while 6 of 11 sectors fell and small caps slipped, underscoring a tech-chase tape rather than broad risk-on. SPY gained +0.27% with 44.7% advancers and defensives_minus_cyclicals at -0.67pp, signaling momentum FOMO in growth against softening internals. With the Software 7 up +6.89% and Mag 7 down -1.92%, leadership rotated inside tech toward software and cloud while broader risk assets stayed cautious.

INDICES — VIX closed at 16.3, below its 20d MA of 17.4 and down on the day. QQQ led at +0.60% followed by SPY +0.27%, RSP +0.18%, DIA +0.13%, IJH -0.08%, IJR -0.14%, and IWM -0.50%. STYLE read LARGE > SMALL by 0.74pp, with small at -0.14% and large at +0.60%. The standout cross-asset move was USO -4.36% versus TLT +0.24%, highlighting an oil dump alongside mild duration bid. Neither SPY nor QQQ is flagged below its 200d MA, so no CTA 200d sell trigger was hit.

BREADTH — Only 46.6% of names sit above their 20d, 57.2% above 50d, and 64.9% above 200d, with adv/dec at 93/115, pct_up 44.7%, and uvol/dvol 0.64. Mag7 median 1d was -1.45% versus ex-Mag7 median -0.20%, with narrow_leadership=True and SPY +0.27% against universe median -0.23% flagging index strong, internals weak.

VOLUME — SPY’s volume sat near average via RSP 0.8x context while QQQ traded lighter at 0.8x, making today’s QQQ +0.60% a low-conviction rally in growth. Elevated sector volume in XLB 2.4x, XLP 1.8x, and XLK 1.5x shows materials and staples under distribution while tech’s +2.48% came with real accumulation. Small caps IJR at 1.8x and IJH 1.1x on red-to-flat prices point to active de-risking in the lower-cap complex.

SECTORS — XLK Technology +2.48%, XLE Energy +1.79%, XLC Comms -0.07%, XLF Financials -0.29%, XLI Industrials -0.42%, XLB Materials -0.45%, XLP Staples -1.06%, XLV Healthcare -1.09%, XLRE Real Estate -1.64%, XLY Discretionary -2.22%, XLU Utilities -2.97%. Top industries ETFs were WCLD +9.10% and DRAM +7.59%, while GDX -3.14% and TAN -3.79% lagged hardest.

ROTATION — Money-in flowed toward software, cloud, and DRAM via WCLD +9.10%, IGV +5.94%, and CIBR +5.74%, while money-out hit utilities, solar, and precious metals with UTES -3.30%, TAN -3.79%, and GDX -3.14%. With defensives_minus_cyclicals at -0.67pp and XLY -2.22% alongside XLU -2.97%, the tape reads as a narrow, growth-centric chase rather than broad cyclicals, so label: narrow mega-cap tape.

THE 7s — Top baskets: Software +6.89%, Cloud +3.17%, Energy +2.42%, Fab +1.84%, Buildout +0.42%. Bottom baskets: Old Metals -2.53%, Defense -2.72%, Power -3.57%, Health -1.66%, Mag -1.92%. In Software, NET was a leader at +6.4% while a weaker name lagged low single digits, and in Cloud, IBM jumped +7.60% while a smaller component underperformed. In Power, CEG plunged -7.66% as the clear laggard while another component held closer to flat, marking sharp internal divergence.

FED & LIQUIDITY — The next FOMC is June 17, 2026 with CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, and 0% hike against a hawk/neutral/dove tally of 6/5/4. USD liquidity stress printed 51/100 (Elevated), with CP−Tbill spread at 0.16% in the 86th percentile as the most-stressed component and Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile. TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $1.9B remain benign against a still-elevated TGA of $781,293M.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 1.19 with skew +3.3% and QQQ PCR_vol 1.35 with skew +8.0% show solid demand for protection, especially in Nasdaq. IWM PCR_vol 2.36 with IV 22.1% signals heavy small-cap hedging as IWM fell -0.50%. With VIX at 16.3 below its 20d 17.4 and MOVE 70.9 below its 20d 75.0, equity and rate vol both sit in muted territory despite put-heavy positioning. SPY near but above its GEX_flip 750.0 and QQQ above 722.0 leaves dealer gamma still dampening, not amplifying, spot moves.

CROSS-ASSET — USO -4.36% and GLD -1.33% alongside HYG -0.06% and TLT +0.24% show a mild risk-off tilt in real assets while credit and duration remain orderly. This combination fits the “tech over everything” equity bid with investors selling inflation and commodity beta rather than expressing macro fear.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 61.6/100 reads as Greed. Biggest individual movers were IBM +7.60% from The Cloud 7, CEG -7.66% from The Power 7, and FDX +1.93% from The Freight 7. On 20d momentum, XLK +20.80% is best sector and XLU -7.05% worst, while Fab +25.40% leads baskets and Power -8.60% lags. SOCL 6.0x vol with +3.38% and PEJ 5.4x vol with +1.40% look like accumulation, while XHE 5.1x vol with -1.07% points to targeted distribution in healthcare. ORCL’s +10.84% move on AI and cloud optimism anchored the broader software stampede.

WATCH — NET +6.4% sits in the Software basket with AI-news-driven strength and stretched 20d momentum, worth watching for either continuation or reversal. IBM +7.60% on 3.5x volume inside Cloud looks like fresh institutional accumulation after its move, key for gauging non-Mag tech leadership. CEG -7.66% with 3.3x volume in the Power basket is a potential canary for further de-risking in utilities and yield-proxy trades.
